subsection.name: Description
subsection.div: Determines complexity of methods, classes and files by counting the Non Commenting Source Statements (NCSS). This check adheres to the specification for the JavaNCSS-Tool written by Chr. Clemens Lee.
subsection.p: Roughly said the NCSS metric is calculated by counting the source lines which are not comments, (nearly) equivalent to counting the semicolons and opening curly braces.
The NCSS for a class is summarized from the NCSS of all its methods, the NCSS of its nested classes and the number of member variable declarations.
The NCSS for a file is summarized from the ncss of all its top level classes, the number of imports and the package declaration.

Rationale: Too large methods and classes are hard to read and costly to maintain. A large NCSS number often means that a method or class has too many responsibilities and/or functionalities which should be decomposed into smaller units.
Here is a breakdown of what exactly is counted and not counted:

tr.td: Each single, static, or wildcard import counts as 1.
Counted at the opening curly brace of the body.
Both static {} and bare {} initializer blocks count as 1.
Each method-like member declaration inside @interface counts as 1. A standalone ; inside @interface also counts as 1.
1 per statement regardless of how many variables are declared on that line. int x, y; counts as 1.
The else keyword counts as 1, separate from the if count.
Every case and default label adds 1.
The try keyword itself does not count.
Each assert statement counts as 1, with or without a message expression.
this() or super() calls inside a constructor body each count as 1.
Statement-level expressions terminated by ; count as 1. A method call inside a return does not add an extra count.
Empty curly braces do not increase the count.
Standalone semicolons outside of @interface do not increase the count.
Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a class.
Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a file including all top level and nested classes.
Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a method.
Specify the maximum allowed number of non commenting lines in a record.

subsection.name: Examples
subsection.p: To configure the check with 4 allowed non commented lines for a method:
To configure the check to set limit of non commented lines in class to 10:
To configure the check to set limit of non commented lines in file to 10:
To configure the check to set limit of non commented lines in record to 5:

subsection.name: Violation Messages
subsection.p: All messages can be customized if the default message doesn't suit you. Please see the documentation to learn how to.
